Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Hartwell" or "Priscilla" or "delivery" or "empty apartment" (or similar) using search_emails to find Priscilla Holt's complaint email (email_hartwell_delivery_complaint) naming Marcus Webb (Chicago) and Sara Dillon (Houston).
OE 2: Search Airtable with a query related to "Completed" or "Swift" or "verbal" (or similar) using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on table tblRelocations01 (base appMoveOpsOps001), not scoped to Hartwell Partners only, to find all 4 affected records across Hartwell Partners and Nexagen Bio.
OE 3: Search Linear for Swift-related tickets using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Swift" or "delivery confirmation" or "photo" (or similar), finding OPS-397 (issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30, status: done, P2).
OE 4: Read OPS-397's comment history using linear_get_issue or linear_list_comments on issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30, OR search Slack #operations (C006) with a query related to "OPS-397" or "photo" or "verbal" or "March 10" or "before" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Noah Reyes's March 17 post warning the fix is prospective only.
OE 5: Search Slack #operations (C006) with a query related to "Nexagen" or "Yuki" or "Reuben" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Chloe Vance's March 18 post identifying Yuki Abe (Austin) and Reuben Torres (Denver) as additionally affected Nexagen Bio employees.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for pending Swift Logistics vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills to find BILL-SW-441 ($2,800, Hartwell deliveries, Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon) and BILL-SW-442 ($3,400, Nexagen deliveries, Yuki Abe and Reuben Torres), both Pending.
OE 7: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue tracking the Swift delivery failure incident, with a title referencing "Swift delivery failure" or "unverified deliveries" and a description noting OPS-397's fix does not cover pre-March 10 bookings, 4 records across 2 clients are affected, and bills BILL-SW-441 and BILL-SW-442 should be held.
OE 8: Add a comment to OPS-397 (issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30) using linear_create_comment documenting that the photo documentation requirement applies only to bookings from March 10 onward, does not cover the 4 affected moves closed on verbal confirmation, and a new tracking ticket has been created.
OE 9: Update all 4 affected Airtable records using airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations01 for record IDs reccaa8a3a0f5754d, rece7ca6a29d32146, rec6abd296209fc42, rec00ae40cbee0842 (or equivalent such as "Pending Verification").
OE 10: Post a message to #operations (C006) using conversations_add_message summarizing the 4 affected employees across Hartwell Partners and Nexagen Bio, explaining that OPS-397 does not cover pre-March 10 moves and that its photo documentation requirement is prospective (March 10+), confirming that Airtable records have been updated, noting that the 4 deliveries are now disputed.
OE 11: Post a message to #vendor-management (channel_id C010) using conversations_add_message, escalating the Swift Logistics SLA breach and summarizing the vendor-level impact: delivery failures, pending bill hold, and the March 22 vendor review context.
OE 12: Reply to Priscilla Holt using reply_to_email (parent: email_hartwell_delivery_complaint) or send_email to priscilla.holt@hartwellpartners.com acknowledging the complaint, naming Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon, and confirming their records have been flagged and the issue is being investigated.
OE 13: Email the Swift vendor billing POC, Priya Kapoor (priya.kapoor@moveops.com, Finance Analyst), using send_email requesting that payment be held on bill BILL-SW-441 ($2,800, Hartwell deliveries, Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon) and bill BILL-SW-442 ($3,400, Nexagen deliveries, Yuki Abe and Reuben Torres) until physical delivery is confirmed.
OE 14: Look up Nexagen Bio in the CRM using crm_search_contacts or crm_search_companies to find Daniel Seo (CRM ID: b0f1d7dff49948d69c0fbcf84b2cee6e, email: daniel.seo@nexagenbio.com, HR Coordinator).
OE 15: Send an email using send_email to daniel.seo@nexagenbio.com (Daniel Seo, HR Coordinator, Nexagen Bio) naming Yuki Abe (Austin) and Reuben Torres (Denver) as the affected employees and notifying them that MoveOps is investigating the delivery status.
OE 16: Use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start 2026-03-21 and end 2026-03-25 to find the March 22 'Weekly vendor review with Swift Logistics' event (event ID: 091d4b8315e3440c83651248d447bf71, 3:00-4:00 PM UTC) on Blessing's calendar.
OE 17: Search MoveOps internal contacts using contacts_search_contacts with a query related to "Swift" or "billing" or "finance" (or similar) to discover Priya Kapoor (priya.kapoor@moveops.com, Finance Analyst), identified in her contact description as the lead point of contact for Swift Logistics billing and vendor management financial matters.
OE 18: Update the March 22 'Weekly vendor review with Swift Logistics' calendar event (event ID: 091d4b8315e3440c83651248d447bf71) using calendar_edit_calendar_event to add a preparation note in the description referencing the Swift delivery failures across 4 employees (Hartwell Partners and Nexagen Bio), the pending bill hold on BILL-SW-441 and BILL-SW-442, and the need to escalate unverified deliveries during the call.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
